person.name: Danny Tran
person.bio: I am a seasoned engineer specializing in building platforms with over 10 years of IT experience, currently driving Istio service mesh and encouraging a vibrant developer culture at National Australia Bank. My technical focus centers on CNCF technologies, with deep specialization in Istio service mesh, Cilium, and Kubernetes service-to-service discovery and security. I am currently writing a dedicated Cilium book specifically for CKS enthusiasts and am an active contributor to the homelab community. You can follow my work and technical deep dives on my blog at dantran.is-a.dev.

person.name: Romain Boulanger
person.bio: Eternally passionate and deeply curious, I’ve always enjoyed learning on my own, particularly anything to do with automation, containerisation and IT security.  I’m also a big fan of open-source technologies, which I experiment with on a daily basis as I set up my own infrastructure to host my personal blog: blog.filador.ch. With a rate of one article a month, I love sharing what I discover!  Finally, I’ve had the opportunity to work with several cloud providers: Google Cloud, AWS, Azure and even AliCloud. If you ask me the question, “Which one do you prefer?” I’d answer that it depends on your use case!  Since 2024, I’ve been an Ambassador for the Silicon Chalet. This Meetup brings together a community of over 1,600 members from Switzerland and France! My role is to promote tools and approaches close to my heart, either by organising events or by presenting topics.

person.name: Ryoji Kobayashi
person.bio: Backend/DevOps engineer with hands-on experience in Linux systems, Golang, Docker, Kubernetes, and MySQL. Interested in operational improvement and automation using CI/CD, ArgoCD, Jenkins, etc., and building secure systems.

person.name: Sander Van Schie
person.bio: I am a devoted engineer, who has a strong passion for public cloud technology focusing on AWS and GCP. I am experienced in Kubernetes, Terraform as well as cloud-native technologies, which makes me excel in creating robust, secure and scalable infrastructures. My strong sense of duty and being quick on my feet are what keep me going all the time.

person.name: Damien Obringer
person.bio: Damien is an experienced Solutions Architect currently working at PwC Luxembourg. He is passionate about designing and implementing scalable, highly available, and maintainable applications using .NET, Kubernetes, Helm, and other Cloud-Native technologies.

person.name: Ivan Kraljek
person.bio: DevOps engineer specializing in infrastructure, containerization and orchestration, security, and monitoring. Passionate about building reliable systems and contributing to the community while continuously learning and evolving.

person.name: Lori Karikari
person.bio: Lori is a Cloud Engineer in Belgium with a passion for Linux, Kubernetes and automation. Outside work, she's hopelessly addicted to her homelab Kubernetes cluster.

person.name: Ricardo Rocha
person.bio: Ricardo leads the Platform Infrastructure team at CERN with a strong focus on cloud native deployments and machine learning. He has led for several years the internal effort to transition services and workloads to use cloud native technologies, as well as dissemination and training efforts. Ricardo got CERN to join the CNCF and is a lead of the CNCF Research User Group. Prior to this work Ricardo helped develop the grid computing infrastructure serving the Large Hadron Collider (LHC).

person.name: Chng Shen Jie
person.bio: Guardian of the New Frontier. Interested in Solution Architecting, DevSecOps and Platform Engineering.

person.name: Dixán Rivas González
person.bio: Since 2007, I’ve worked across Linux, Active Directory, networking and Kubernetes. Curiosity at my core, I push for systems that are reliable, stable, and built to last.
